Burlington Anti-ICE Coalition 2

Burlington Groups
  • FixICE.org
  • Bearing Witness New England
  • Justice4All
  • Burn Resilience Network
  • No Fear Street Theater
  • Action Network De-ICE Hanscom
Weekly & Special Events
  • MONDAY & TUESDAY, 10am-1pm: FIX ICE, 1000 District Ave. fixice.org/mondaytuesday
  • WEDNESDAYS 11am-1pm: BearingWitness @ ICE Burlington, 1000 District Ave. bearingwitnessNE.org
  • THURSDAY & FRIDAY 11am-1pm: Justice 4 All @ ICE Burlington, 1000 District Ave. Facebook
  • EVERY OTHER FRIDAY, 4:30-6:30pm: B.U.R.N. Black Tie Soirée Series, Café Escadrille, 26 Cambridge St. burnresilience.network/BTSS/
  • SATURDAYS 10am-2pm: ICEWeekendWatch, 1000 District Ave. tinyurl.com/mutcubd
  • SATURDAYS 11am-1pm: No Fear Street Theatre, Cnr. of Mall Rd. & District Ave. nofearstreettheater.com
What is happening?

The Burlington ICE Facility (BIF) is the regional ICE headquarters for all of New England. Originally an administrative office and short-term processing center, this unassuming office building has now been turned into an illegal prison.

Every detainee in New England is put through this facility, sometimes kept for days or weeks, made to endure without basics like beds, showers, medical care, or meals. They are packed so tightly that they must take turns lying down on the floor, and the cell lights are kept on at all hours, making sleep nigh impossible. When they are lucky enough to get food, it is too often spoiled or contaminated.

Legal representatives, family, and community members are frequently left stranded outside the facility, unable to make contact with those inside, even when trying to deliver lifesaving medication. Right behind the Burlington Mall!

Quick History
  • 2007: Robert Murray leases the building to ICE. DHS promises the Town of Burlington that no one will be held in this facility for more than 1-2hrs, with no overnights. Facility opens. [1]
  • May, 2025: Detainees first report being held for long stretches without beds, showers or meals, including teenager Marcelo Gomez da Silva, held for six days and given crackers for meals. [2]
  • Ongoing: Detainees, lawyers, and congresspeople who have visited the building confirm reports of overcrowding and poor treatment. [3]
Isn't that illegal?

It is indeed illegal! This facility violates everything from Burlington Town zoning to state fire code to the Constitution (as people are held without due process in inhumane conditions). [4]

Burlington Town has tried to inspect the facility and has been to court, so far with no success. [5] Congresspeople have also toured or attempted to tour the facility and have agreed that conditions are inhumane and unacceptable. [6]

The facility is owned by a private landlord, Robert Murray. Murray stands to make $2.2 million in rent from this facility in 2026. [7]

What can I do about it?
  • Spread the word: Tell your friends, family, and coworkers about this illegal facility. Visit burnresilience.network/Zines for resources, flyers, and a downloadable zine.
  • Share online: Share the info page on social media!
  • Call your reps: Ask what their plan is to shut this facility down, and encourage them to perform inspections. Script and list of MA reps: burnresilience.network/Call
  • Protest: See the events list above and bring a friend!
  • Become a safety marshal: No experience needed, just a two-hour class! Training: mobilize.us/indivisiblesomerville/event/881283/
  • Donate food: Food donations accepted 10am-1pm Monday-Friday. Or donate via Venmo to @Toni-M.
Organizations & Actions

FixICE.org: Exposes the reality of the ICE detention center at 1000 District Ave. in Burlington. Protests every Monday & Tuesday, 10 AM – 1 PM. Mission: empower people to stop ICE cruelties, support immigrants, and win elections for lasting change.

Bearing Witness: Peaceful protest outside the ICE office, supporting immigrants and expressing outrage over ICE’s illegal and cruel behavior. Wednesdays, 11am-1pm. Includes guest speakers, food/resource distribution, and a peaceful march. bearingwitnessne.org

Justice 4 All: Weekly protest at the ICE Facility every Thursday & Friday, 11 AM – 1 PM, organized by Greater Assabet Indivisible Network (GAIN). Live music, standout with signs, march, and speakers sharing local immigrant stories. Goal: stop abuse and demand justice for all.

Black Tie Soirée Series: Protest at Café Escadrille, owned by Robert Murray (ICE landlord), every other Friday, 4:30 PM – 6 PM. Highlights Murray’s role in bringing ICE to Burlington and ongoing abuses. burnresilience.network/BTSS

No Fear Street Theater: Creative protest with open mic, poetry, speeches, and music. Saturdays, 11 AM – 1 PM. Supports Weekend Watch. nofearstreettheater.com

Weekend Watch: Vigil-style action creating a safe space for immigrants and families outside BIF on Saturdays, 10AM–2PM. Quiet, supportive, and community-focused.

Sources

  5. burnresilience.network/ICEcadrille/Documentation/ (#3 & #4)
  6. wbur.org/news/2025/06/11/inhumanedetainments-burlington-ice-dhs-women
  7. burnresilience.network/ICEcadrille/Documentation/
